wounded, brought back to Driftmark
died

title: The Red Dragon and the Gold

by dragon (Vhagar)
flew in independently on Sunfyre
killed with Meleys
returned to Dragonstone after making pact with Freys
marching with the Green army to Harrenhal

marches out with Criston Cole's Green army toward Harrenhal
sails to the Free Cities to treat with the Triarchy

after Seasmoke claims him at Hull, flies to Dragonstone and bends the knee to Rhaenyra
answer the call to Dragonstone and claim Vermithor and Silverwing at the Red Sowing

secretly traveled to Dragonstone to negotiate terms with Rhaenyra

title: Salt and Sea, Fire and Blood

corlys.note: at sea with the Velaryon fleet

roderick.note: leads the Winter Wolves; routs the Lannister host at the Red Fork alongside Daemon

oscar.note: Lord of Riverrun, commanding the river host at the Red Fork

alysanne.note: her Blackwood archers fight at the Red Fork

alys.note: warns the dragonseeds to abandon Harrenhal before Aemond descends

movements.note: leads the Velaryon fleet against the Triarchy at the Battle of the Gullet; High Tide is sacked
fly Vermax and Moondancer to the Gullet to break the Triarchy assault
killed at the Gullet after Vermax is downed by a scorpion bolt and drowns
Moondancer survives; returns to Dragonstone after the battle
captured by Rhaenyra's forces and brought to Dragonstone as hostages
having bonded the wild dragon Sheepstealer, flies to the Battle of the Gullet
fights at the Gullet with the Velaryon fleet and slays Sharako Lohar
arrives with the Triarchy fleet
leads the Triarchy fleet to the Gullet
killed by Alyn of Hull at the Gullet

daemon.note: encamped with the Winter Wolves by the Gods Eye

otto.note: revealed: Larys had him locked in the Black Cells beneath the Red Keep all along

corlys.note: missing — cast into the sea by Lohar during the battle

criston.note: his host advances on Harrenhal through the Riverlands

alyn.note: combing the wreckage for his father

ormund.note: musters the Hightower host north along the Roseroad

tyland.note: adrift among the Triarchy's shattered fleet

roderick.note: the Winter Wolves crush a Lannister host by the Gods Eye lakeshore

oscar.note: the river host celebrates the rout

alysanne.note: her archers among the lakeshore victors

luthor.note: gold cloak officer — his City Watch holds the capital

jeyne.note: Lady of the Eyrie and the Vale

movements.note: flies Moondancer to the battle wreckage to recover Jace's body
bears Jace's body home to a shattered Rhaenyra
finish off the Triarchy remnants still afloat in the strait
washed ashore, found alive on the rocks — 'If this be victory, I pray I never win another'
search the shoreline for their father; the recovered Corlys grants Alyn the Velaryon name
flies Sheepstealer to the Vale; Jeyne Arryn grudgingly grants asylum, wary of the wild dragon
Triarchy stragglers ambush their escort mid-transfer; the pair slip their chains and vanish into the Crownlands
burns Harrenhal and puts Simon Strong's line to the sword; a dying man's knife leaves him gravely wounded — Alys Rivers hides him
rally at Dragonstone for the assault on the capital
flies to Rhaenyra's side with news of Jace's death; Alys names it 'the beginning of the end'
four dragons over the city; Luthor Largent's gold cloaks open the gates and the garrison stands down on Alicent's order
beheaded by Rhaenyra in the throne room, Larys's parting 'gift'

criston.note: his host nears the ruined castle

mysaria.note: Mistress of Whisperers in the new court

aemond.note: gravely wounded, hidden by Alys Rivers — both courts believe him lost

baela.note: the court removes to the capital

rhaena.note: asylum in the Vale, kept at arm's length from the Eyrie

tyland.note: adrift among the Triarchy's shattered fleet

daeron.note: with Ormund's host — the 'Daeron' about to be handed over is a decoy

torrhen.note: come south from White Harbor; catches the queen's eye at the rat feast

movements.note: the Hightower host camps on the Roseroad; by episode's end it doubles back, ambushes the Dragonkeepers and seizes Tumbleton — capturing Tessarion
three dragons descend on Ormund's camp; he bends the knee and yields 'Daeron' as hostage
fly home with the hostage — a bleach-haired commoner decoy, later exposed by Alicent
scouts for Vhagar and Sheepstealer; finds only Sunfyre's rotting carcass (site inferred)
brings her grim find to the small council

criston.note: reaches the ruined Harrenhal at last — only Alys Rivers remains, claiming Aemond is gone

aegon.note: fugitive in the Crownlands, seeking Sunfyre

aemond.note: hidden in the depths of Harrenhal, mending

helaena.note: prisoner in the Red Keep — with child

rhaena.note: slips away to hide with Sheepstealer in the Mountains of the Moon

gwayne.note: at Cole's side in the ruins

tyland.note: hunting rumors of the fugitive king

daeron.note: revealed at Ormund's side — his first true appearance

alys.note: swears to Cole that Aemond has fled — a lie

movements.note: resigns as Hand when Rhaenyra refuses to legitimize his sons and sails against the Triarchy's remnants; Alyn serves as Hand in his stead
collects the promised Vale gold from Jeyne Arryn, who needles him about Rhea Royce
Caraxes scents Sheepstealer — Daemon finds Rhaena in her mountain cave, then kills a shepherd and passes the charred skull off as the dead Gullet rider; only Mysaria marks the lie
the fugitives reach the field where Sunfyre fell; Aegon swears his dragon still lives
sent to watch the occupied town — where Hugh's wife Kat lives; Daeron executes her brother as Ormund's loyalty test

corlys.note: at sea against the Triarchy's remnants

aegon.note: hiding in the woods off the Rook's Rest battlefield

aemond.note: recovering — slaughters the bounty hunters who come for Alys

helaena.note: prisoner in the Red Keep — with child

baela.note: flies with Addam on the Vhagar hunt, over villages burned near Wendish Town

rhaena.note: hiding with Sheepstealer in the Mountains of the Moon

addam.note: reports Vhagar's trail to the council; sent hunting her with Baela over the Riverlands

daeron.note: Ormund has declared him king, minting coin in his image

roderick.note: the Winter Wolves join the southbound march

oscar.note: readies the river host to march south

joffrey.note: named heir to the Iron Throne after Jace's death; summoned to court

movements.note: the river host and Winter Wolves march south for Tumbleton; Oscar sends Petyr Piper ahead to seize the Stoney Sept crossing
shadow the column and raid it by night — Cole kills Piper in his bedroll and burns the crossing camp
refuses Criston's suicidal assault on the host and abandons him for Ormund's army
comes ashore hunting rumors and finds the fugitive king — Aegon kills the sellsword Janos in a quarrel over his crown
massacred with his gold cloaks at Daemon's staged 'Feast for Traitors' — the Watch had been bought with Ormund's counterfeit coin
